stories.title: Sabin Middle School Students Head to VEX Robotics World Championship
stories.angle: Three D11 eighth graders earned a spot at the VEX Robotics World Championship in St. Louis (April 25-27) after a 15-award season — a feel-good COS achievement story to lead the day.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Three D11 8th graders from Sabin Middle School are heading to the VEX Robotics World Championship in St. Louis this week. 🤖

Declan Martin, Riley Snyder, and Jaxon Wright. 15 awards. 600 teams. 55 countries.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
COS shoutout: Sabin Middle School 8th graders Declan Martin, Riley Snyder, and Jaxon Wright are at VEX Robotics Worlds in St. Louis — April 25-27. 🤖

600 teams. 55 countries. Go get it.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Three Colorado Springs kids are about to compete on one of the biggest stages in robotics — and they're from your neighborhood. 🤖

Declan Martin, Riley Snyder, and Jaxon Wright are eighth graders at Sabin Middle School in D11. This week they head to St. Louis for the VEX Robotics World Championship — specifically the VEX V5 Middle School division, which runs April 25-27 at the America's Center Convention Complex. They'll be competing against approximately 600 teams from 55 countries.

The three students have been competing since August and brought home 15 awards this season. That's not a typo. This wasn't a fluke — it was a full season of work that earned them a spot on the world stage.

VEX Robotics competitions are engineering challenges where teams design, build, and program robots to complete specific tasks. At the championship level, the field is full of teams who've been preparing for months, many from countries with deep national investment in robotics education. Sabin's team earned their place the same way everyone else did: by being good.

COS parents — do you follow local student robotics competitions? Drop a 🤖 in the comments to cheer them on this week.

---
facebook.image_caption: Three Sabin Middle School 8th graders from D11 are competing at the VEX Robotics World Championship in St. Louis this week. 600 teams. 55 countries. All the COS pride. 🤖

article.headline: Three Sabin Middle School Students Are Heading to the VEX Robotics World Championship

stories.title: Taylor Schreiner Named 2026 Military Child of the Year (Space Force)
stories.angle: Discovery Canyon High School senior honored at a gala in Arlington, VA today — CNA certified at 17, 13 military moves, sister Hayley was 2023 winner. Compelling human interest story with strong COS military community tie-in.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
COS teen Taylor Schreiner named 2026 Military Child of the Year for the Space Force.

Discovery Canyon HS senior. 13 military moves. CNA certified at 17. Gala tonight in Arlington, VA. 🎖️

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Taylor Schreiner's story: 13 military moves, two deployments of her dad, CNA license at 17, and now — Military Child of the Year 2026. 🌟

Colorado Springs, we see her.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Meet Taylor Schreiner — Colorado Springs' own 2026 Military Child of the Year for the Space Force. 🌟

Taylor is a senior at Discovery Canyon High School, 17 years old, and was selected from over 1,000 nominees and 68 finalists representing all seven branches of the U.S. military. The award comes from Operation Homefront and is one of the most meaningful recognitions a military family can receive.

Her story is remarkable. Her father, Brig. Gen. Robert Schreiner, has served 28 years in the Space Force — and Taylor has lived through 13 permanent change-of-station moves in her lifetime. Her dad's deployments have totaled 65 months, meaning he was away for nearly a third of her life. And through all of it, Taylor didn't just adapt — she thrived.

She earned her CNA (certified nursing assistant) license at 17 and spent two years volunteering at Children's Hospital. Her goal is to become a pediatric oncologist. She's planning to attend the University of Wisconsin-Madison to study biology on a pre-med track.

Her sister Hayley was the 2023 Military Child of the Year for the same branch. Tonight, at a gala in Arlington, Virginia, Taylor accepts the 2026 award — along with a $10,000 educational grant from Operation Homefront.

We love seeing COS families recognized like this. What an incredible young person. 💙

---
facebook.image_caption: Discovery Canyon HS senior Taylor Schreiner was named 2026 Military Child of the Year for the Space Force. 13 military moves. CNA license at 17. Gala tonight in Arlington, VA. We're so proud. 💙

article.headline: Discovery Canyon HS Senior Taylor Schreiner Named 2026 Military Child of the Year for the Space Force

stories.title: Bear Creek Earth Day Story Walk and Upcycled Craft — Today
stories.angle: Free, family-friendly Earth Day event happening right now — walk a self-guided story trail, then make a take-home upcycled craft at the nature center.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
TODAY at Bear Creek Nature Center 🌿
Earth Day Story Walk + Upcycled Craft
10 AM – 1 PM MT | Free | All ages
245 Bear Creek Rd, Colorado Springs

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Looking for something to do this afternoon with the family? Bear Creek Nature Center has you covered — and it's free. 🌿

Today from 10 AM to 1 PM, Bear Creek Nature Center is hosting an Earth Day Story Walk and Upcycled Craft. The story walk is self-guided — you and your kids follow a children's story posted along a trail in the nature center, reading pages as you walk. After the trail, you head inside for a take-home craft made from upcycled materials.

It's low-key, outdoors, and genuinely fun for young kids who love both nature and storytime. Bear Creek Nature Center is at 245 Bear Creek Rd in Colorado Springs — it's a beautiful spot even on a regular day, and free events like this make it even better.

If you're reading this at lunchtime and wondering what to do this afternoon, this is your sign.

Have you taken your kids to Bear Creek Nature Center? What's your favorite thing to do there?

---
facebook.image_caption: Today only: Earth Day Story Walk + Upcycled Craft at Bear Creek Nature Center. Free. 10 AM – 1 PM. 245 Bear Creek Rd, COS. 🌿

stories.title: PPYMCA Summer Outdoor Pools Open Memorial Day at Two COS Locations
stories.angle: Monument Valley and Wilson Ranch pools open Memorial Day weekend through Labor Day — advance notice for families planning summer swim memberships and day passes.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
PPYMCA summer pools open Memorial Day – Labor Day at two COS locations: Monument Valley and Wilson Ranch.

Day passes: $10 youth (ages 3–17), $15 adults, $45 family, free ages 2 and under. 🏊

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Summer swim season in Colorado Springs is almost here — and it's time to start planning. 🏊

The PPYMCA opens its two outdoor pools on Memorial Day weekend in partnership with the City of Colorado Springs. Monument Valley Pool and Wilson Ranch Pool will run through Labor Day, giving families a full summer of swimming at two of the most established outdoor pools in the area.

Here's what you need to know before you go:

Day passes are available on the day of your visit (credit card or exact cash only). Pricing: $10 for youth ages 3-17, $15 for adults, $45 for a family pass (two adults plus up to four children). Kids age two and under swim free. Season passes are also available for members and nonmembers if your family swims regularly — worth it if you plan to go more than a handful of times.

Monument Valley Pool is in central Colorado Springs near Monument Valley Park. Wilson Ranch Pool is on the northwest side. Both have been family staples for years and fill up fast on hot summer days, so arriving early matters, especially on weekends.

For current pricing and to purchase season passes in advance, visit ppymca.org.

Are you a season pass family or a day-pass family? Let us know in the comments. 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: PPYMCA summer pools open Memorial Day — Monument Valley and Wilson Ranch pools, through Labor Day. Day passes from $10. Season passes available at ppymca.org. 🏊

article.headline: PPYMCA Summer Outdoor Pools Open Memorial Day — Everything COS Families Need to Know

stories.title: Money Museum National Coin Week Free Open House — This Saturday
stories.angle: Free family outing this Saturday at the Edward C. Rochette Money Museum — games, exhibits, and hands-on activities as part of National Coin Week. Low-lift, high-value local event coverage.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Free this Saturday in Colorado Springs 🪙

Edward C. Rochette Money Museum
National Coin Week Open House
April 25 | 10:30 AM – 5 PM MT
818 N. Cascade Ave

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Looking for a free Saturday outing this weekend? The Edward C. Rochette Money Museum is throwing open its doors — and there's no ticket required. 🪙

This Saturday, April 25, from 10:30 AM to 5 PM, the Money Museum at 818 N. Cascade Ave is hosting a National Coin Week Open House. The event is completely free and includes games, exhibits, and family-friendly activities. This year's National Coin Week theme is "Striking Independence: 250 Years of American Numismatics" — meaning everything ties into the story of American money and history through the country's 250th anniversary year.

The Edward C. Rochette Money Museum is run by the American Numismatic Association, and it's genuinely one of Colorado Springs' most underrated family destinations. The collection includes rare coins, currency, medals, and artifacts that span centuries of monetary history. Kids who are curious about history, design, or collecting tend to love it.

It's free. It's this Saturday. It's indoors if the weather turns. Honestly, we'd go even without a special event.

Will you be checking out the Money Museum this weekend? 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Free Saturday family outing: Money Museum National Coin Week Open House. April 25, 10:30 AM – 5 PM. 818 N. Cascade Ave, Colorado Springs. No ticket needed. 🪙

article.headline: The Money Museum Is Hosting a Free Open House This Saturday — Here's Why Your Family Should Go
